choices.text: Trim or summarize history. Trim: keep recent N messages (lose old). Summarize: LLM condenses old messages into summary; keep recent verbatim. Pattern: when len(messages) > threshold, replace old with summary + recent. For cross-session memory: vector DB stores extracted facts; retrieve relevant at query time. LangMem library auto-extracts + updates memories. Always implement: context overflow inevitable in long conversations.

questions.explanation: Trim recent + summarize old. Vector DB for long-term memory.

questions.prompt: User returns next day; agent doesn't remember previous conversation. Fix?

choices.text: Add checkpointer + thread_id for state persistence. SqliteSaver or PostgresSaver stores state per thread_id; resume mid-conversation across sessions. `from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite import SqliteSaver; memory = SqliteSaver.from_conn_string('memory.db'); app = graph.compile(checkpointer=memory); app.invoke(input, {'configurable': {'thread_id': 'user_123'}})`. Same thread_id resumes; new thread_id starts fresh. Combined with long-term memory in vector DB: agent has conversation history + persistent facts about user. Production agents always implement persistence; without it, fresh session every invocation.

questions.explanation: Checkpointer (SqliteSaver/PostgresSaver) + thread_id. State persists across sessions per thread.
